message: Jake
the man in the red suit
Günter
That day, I was
called out by the doctor on the phone.
Unusually, I was told there was something to discuss regarding Kristy, so I came here, but I wonder what kind of talk it is?
Sorry for calling you out so early in the morning.
No problem.
Such things
have always looked at me with the same cold eyes, contradicting their words. What an expressionless man.
I always think.
He was deep in thought, contemplating something with a serious face, but finally opened his mouth.
Under normal circumstances, I would think it's a mistake to call you for something like this, but the situation is what it is.
Why does this man speak in such a wrapped-up manner? This kind of phrasing is
for a moment, it felt like something stirred at the bottom of my memory. It seems I've spoken with a man like this before.
Is something the matter?
No, it's nothing.
Hmm.
Both of us are pressed for time, so let's get right to the point.
Look
Talk
Receive questions
Hear
Think
System
The surroundings
About Kristy
About Kristy
the matter of
Save
End the game
The walls and floor are unified in dark colors, and the shelves are filled with books that appear to be medical literature. The only decoration is a green pot by the window.
I've never had to see a psychiatrist myself, but I wonder how they can treat patients in such a gloomy room?
Being cramped in a place like this would probably make my nerves even worse.
It's a terribly plain, gloomy room.
Can anyone relax and work in a room like this?
In his early 30s, of medium build, with a slightly receding hairline. Maybe it's due to stress from his job?
Of European descent.
He might look like a Jew.
He's just an ordinary type. One could say he's conservative, but on the flip side, he has no distinguishing features.
That lofty attitude typical of doctors and lawyers who look down on others.
I can't say I like him much.
Well, I've finished asking you questions. Thanks for your cooperation.
I was called out without warning and ended up with a one-sided questioning.
Not understanding what was happening, I was tossed out once they were done with me.
He's a thoroughly disgusting man.
I was pretty irritated. I’ve never liked being pushed around by others.
That's why I work alone.
At that moment, there was a man standing in my way.
The man was blocking my path.
Sorry, but I want to go that way.
You.
- Snipes?
So what?
Huff.
Mm, you.
The man, chewing gum obnoxiously, continued to block my way.
Hmph.
Without saying anything, the man stood in my way, blocking my path and chewing gum with a smirk. What is with this guy?
If you have nothing for me, could you move aside?
Well, I don't know.
Well, you see!? I'm busy, so just disappear somewhere before you get angry.
Hehehe.
Hey, can't you hear me? If you've got something to say, hurry it up. If not, get out of the way.
Only if I feel like it.
Crunch, crunch.
As always, the man stands in front of me. He doesn't do anything, just blocking my way.
I take a good look at the man in front of me. Wearing a hat and sunglasses, he wouldn't win any awards for fashion.
In a cheap-looking satin suit and a flashy patterned shirt. That tie really makes you wonder where he found it.
Yeah, yeah, I get why you'd want to stare. I've got incredible taste, right?
You sure are.
Hm.
So, what the hell does that even mean?
I was getting more and more annoyed and thought that if I was confident in my arms, I would have thrown a punch immediately.
I thought.
But luckily for the guy in front of me, I'm not good at that kind of thing.
Well, today, it's just to show my face. I'll come properly to greet you later.
What kind of greeting does this guy think he's going to do?
If you want to greet me, you can do it right now.
You really don't get it, do you?
You really don't.
It's natural to not understand. You seem to know me, but I don't know you.
Doesn't that just mean you're famous? Good for you. Hehehe.
Hey, why don't you say something?
Something.
Oh, I see. You're going to keep acting like that.
Well then, we might meet again. If we both stay alive.
The man left some troubling words behind. Just who the heck is he?
I'm really tired; I should probably go back to the office.
He seemed to be pondering something for a moment. Then, as he met my gaze, he opened his mouth as if resolved.
The reason I called you here today is none other than regarding Miss Dern.
Yes.
Miss Dern has been steadily recovering since she came to me. That's what I want you to acknowledge.
What on earth happened to Kristy?
Well, there's no need to rush.
There's plenty of time left.
Now, I have a couple of questions for you, is that alright?
They’re about Kristy, right?
Of course.
Understood. I will cooperate as much as I can.
Thank you very much.
For now, I want you to answer my questions.
Thank you for your cooperation.
No problem.
But, is what we've discussed beneficial for Kristy?
I am not wrong in what I'm doing.
You can leave it to me, I'll provide her with the best treatment.
First, I want to confirm something.
I heard from Miss Dern that you were her boyfriend. Is that true?
We were boyfriend and girlfriend in school. But does that relate to the current situation?
I can't say that it doesn't. In any circumstance, it might hold significance for her.
Let’s be frank. Have you had sexual relations with Kristy?
That's quite a sudden question, I'm a bit taken aback.
I’m not asking out of curiosity or prying.
The answer is yes.
Hmm, I see.
Alright. So, how was she during sex?
Huh, what do you mean by that?
For example, she was extremely timid, or perhaps she liked things to be quite intense.
That kind of thing.
You're asking something that's hard to answer.
Would it be better to hypnotize you and find out while you're asleep?
No, that's not necessary. She preferred very typical sex.
It wasn't SM or anything abnormal; nor did she seem to be afraid of sex, it was all quite normal.
Was she always normal? Did she ever show extreme resistance or dislike?
I can't say it was completely absent.
So, there was at least some.
Wait a second. There are various situations, right? Sometimes little things can cause things to go wrong.
Yeah, yeah, I get it. But I wouldn't say her peril was never revealed in all of that.
If that's the case, anyone would behave that way.
That's correct in a sense. The human heart is fragile.
Once you put it that way, I can't say anything.
Well, that's a pretty natural reaction.
So, do you not remember how she spoke about her family?
Family.
She was not very keen on talking about her family.
She was a daughter of the Dern family. The child of a couple that was living apart without divorce.
Did you not feel she had a dislike for men?
If that were the case, she wouldn’t have gone out with me.
However, because you were persistent, she reluctantly started dating.
In that case, that's a different story.
I don't think she seemed reluctant at all.
Is that so? We really don't know what it was like in reality, do we?
It's been a while, so I don't remember the details.
Why did you switch to him from me, I wonder?
Well, that's something only she knows.
Yeah, that's probably the case.
But you know.
Could that possibly lead to her symptoms improving?
We can't deny that even the smallest things might hold meaning for her.
Alright, that's the end of the questions.
It's true that she has recovered from the emotional turmoil regarding her fiancé and the gossip.
What do you mean by that?
Well, new anxieties have arisen.
In any case, I would like you to turn the tables and ask the questions now.
Actually, her mental state is very precarious; when I listen to her, she talks about dreaming of killing people.
That's what she says.
Dreaming of killing people?
Yes, she says she dreams about cutting people up.
No way.
I think it's unlikely, but I can't completely deny it in her current state.
No way, just because she's had such dreams means she's actually doing it?
I can't completely rule that out, not with her right now.
As long as she is my patient, I have an obligation to do my best for her.
That's my duty.
Are you suggesting that Kristy has been mentally ill and might have committed murder as a result?
Who would believe such a story?
Is it true that anything trivial is important to her now; is she really that mentally unwell?
That Dern family.
People talk about Kristy's family like that.
Kristy's mother's family was wealthy bankers, and her father was taken in as a son-in-law.
However, their marriage did not last long, and when Kristy was 8, they separated.
However, divorce is never permitted to avoid leaving a stain on the family tree, and despite being married for over fifteen years, they continue to live separately.
What has now happened to Kristy?
What on earth is this man, who doesn't change a single expression, thinking?
My expression is probably changing slightly. If he’s secretly smirking at that,
I'm feeling unwell.
He boldly asks the hard questions.
Even if it's for treatment,
it feels like I'm being interrogated.
I,
was irritated by his attitude. His demeanor was consistently that of an arrogant man.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
